title: Calonica

{{Quote|''[…]'' a spot inspection on Calonica found that fewer than 10 percent of the 50,000 LX-2 mines, buried around the Imperial capital, contained explosive charges.|A compilation of Galactic Empire documents by Major Arhul Hextrophon|<ref name="ISB2" />}}
'''Calonica''' was an [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] celestial body located in the [[Vensensor sector/Legends|Vensensor sector]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] it was the site of a [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Unidentified Imperial capital|capital]] that was surrounded by fifty thousand buried [[LX-2 comlink mine]]s. At one point, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] managed to remotely deactivate the [[Mine/Legends|mines]] and then proceeded to steal the [[Explosive charge/Legends|explosive charges]] from more than ninety percent of them, a fact that eventually led to the development of the [[LX-4 proton mine/Legends|LX-4 proton mine]].

==Description==
Calonica was a terrestrial astronomical object<ref name="ISB2" /> located in the [[Calonica system/Legends|Calonica system]], a part of the [[Vensensor sector/Legends|Vensensor sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was situated on the [[Corellian Trade Spine/Legends|Corellian Trade Spine]] [[Super-hyperroute/Legends|super-hyperroute]], which connected it to the [[Tar Morden system/Legends|Tar Morden system]]<ref name="TEA" /> as well as the [[Bomis Koori system/Legends|Bomis Koori system]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="ExpansionRegion">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-extra-expansion-region|text=Essential Atlas Extra: Expansion Region}}</ref>

==History==
{{Quote|A follow-up investigation revealed that Rebel spies had somehow discovered the mine's secret com code, and were using it to deactivate the mines. Once deactivated, the Rebels dug them up, stole their charges, and then buried the mines back in the ground.|A compilation of Galactic Empire documents by Major Arhul Hextrophon|<ref name="ISB2" />}}
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] had a presence on Calonica, with an [[Unidentified Imperial capital|Imperial capital]] there having fifty thousand [[Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc./Legends|Merr-Sonn Munitions]] [[LX-2 comlink mine]]s buried around it. At some point, [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Spy/Legends|spies]] managed to remotely deactivate the [[Mine/Legends|mines]], after which Rebels dug them up, stole the [[Explosive charge/Legends|explosive charges]] from more than ninety percent of the mines, and then re-buried them.<ref name="ISB2" />

A spot inspection subsequently discovered the lack of charges, which eventually led to the development of the [[LX-4 proton mine/Legends|LX-4 proton mine]] that could only be deactivated by detonating it. Shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the incident on Calonica was mentioned in a compilation of Imperial documents made by Rebel [[Historian/Legends|historian]] [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Arhul Hextrophon/Legends|Arhul Hextrophon]] for Alliance [[Supreme Commander (Alliance)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] and other Rebel [[Officer/Legends|officers]].<ref name="ISB2" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:TauSekar-VoyagesSF13.png|thumb|right|250px|The article "Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are" placed Calonica in the Tau Sekar sector.]]
Calonica was first mentioned in [[Greg Gorden]]'s [[1989]] ''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'' that was published by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="ISB">''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]''</ref> "[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[roleplaying game]] source article published in the [[Voyages SF 13|thirteenth issue]] of the ''[[Voyages SF]]'' magazine, placed Calonica in the [[Tau Sekar sector]].<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> The article is considered [[Canon|noncanonical]] with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="Unlicensed">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently placed the Calonica [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the celestial body, in grid square K-16.<ref name="TEA" />
